Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Deconstruct and Understand the Data I need to parse the event log Case 2001 Submit 0900 Evaluate 0930 Approve 1000 Pay 1015 Close 1030 Total duration 1 5 hours Attributes Resource varies Jane Mike Ann Alan Jane Region A Complexity Low Case 2002 Submit 0905 Evaluate 0945 Req Add Docs 1400 Approve Next day 1000 Pay 1045 Close 1100 Total duration 26 hours Attributes Resource varies Paul Lisa Lisa Ann Carl Paul Region B Complexity Medium Case 2003 Submit 0910 Evaluate 0940 Req Add Docs 1100 Req Add Docs 1700 Approve Next day 1600 Pay Next next day 0900 Close 0930 Total duration 48 5 hours Attributes Resource varies Jane Mike Mike Mike Bill Alan Jane Region A Complexity High Case 2004 Submit 0920 Evaluate 0935 Approve 1000 Pay 1030 Close 1045 Total duration 1 5 hours Attributes Resource varies Mary Lisa Ann Carl Mary Region B Complexity Low Case 2005 Submit 0925 Evaluate 0950 Req Add Docs 1130 Req Add Docs Next day 1700 Req Add Docs Next next day 1500 Approve Next next next day 1000 Pay 1400 Close 1430 Total duration 77 hours Attributes Resource varies Paul Lisa Lisa Lisa Lisa Bill Carl Paul Region B Complexity High

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Calculate Case Durations and Identify Outliers Case 2001 1 5 hours Fast Case 2002 26 hours Medium Slow Lets compare Case 2003 48 5 hours Slow Case 2004 1 5 hours Fast Case 2005 77 hours Very Slow Comparison Cases 2001 and 2004 are very fast 1 5 hours Cases 2002 2003 and 2005 are significantly slower

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: Root Cause Hypothesis 1 Is it the Request Additional Documents activity 2001 No 2002 Yes 2003 Yes 2004 No 2005 Yes Conclusion Cases with Request Additional Documents are slower This is a strong indicator

Name: Inductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Analyze Attributes against Duration Complexity Low 2001 1 5h 2004 1 5h Fast Medium 2002 26h Slow High 2003 48 5h 2005 77h Very Slow Correlation Higher complexity correlates strongly with longer duration High complexity cases often trigger Request Additional Documents

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Region Region A 2001 1 5h Low 2003 48 5h High Region B 2002 26h Med 2004 1 5h Low 2005 77h High Analysis Region A has both fast and slow Region B has fast medium and slow Conclusion Region doesnt seem to be the primary driver The performance difference is likely due to Complexity not Region If Region were the cause all Region A or all Region B cases would behave similarly They dont

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Resource Observation Resources change per activity Resource attribute is per event Evaluation Who evaluates 2001 Mike Low Fast 2002 Lisa Med Slow 2003 Mike High Slow 2004 Lisa Low Fast 2005 Lisa High Slow Analysis Lisa handles Med Slow and High Slow but also Low Fast Mike handles Low Fast and High Slow Conclusion The resource doesnt correlate with slowness Lisa is fast on Low slow on Med High Mike is fast on Low slow on High The slowness tracks with the case attributes Complexity not the specific person

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Note Request Additional Documents is done by Lisa in 2002 2005 and Mike in 2003 Since Mike and Lisa both do this and it happens on Med High cases the bottleneck is likely the activity itself or the complexity not the specific resource doing the request

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Deep Dive into Root Causes Complexity High Medium complexity claims require more work This manifests as Request Additional Documents Request Additional Documents This activity introduces waiting time 2002 Eval to Req is 4 5 hours Req to Approve is 20 hours 2003 Eval to Req is 1 5 hours Req to Req is 6 hours Req to Approve is 23 hours 2005 Eval to Req is 1 5 hours Req to Req is 29 5 hours Req to Req is 22 hours Req to Approve is 19 hours Observation The Request Additional Documents activity seems to consume the bulk of the time The time between requests or after the last request before approval is long

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Hypothesis The delay is likely due to the customer response time or the internal processing of the additional documents The log doesnt explicitly show Customer Response but the gap between Request and Approve or next Request suggests a waiting period

Name: Inductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Specifics Case 2005 has three requests This suggests a cycle of incomplete information This is a major performance killer Case 2003 has two requests Case 2002 has one Correlation Number of requests increases with complexity 2002 Med 1 request 2003 High 2 requests 2005 High 3 requests Conclusion Higher complexity leads to more requests which leads to exponential growth in duration

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Synthesize Findings Cases with issues 2002 2003 2005 Primary Root Cause Claim Complexity High Medium complexity triggers Request Additional Documents Secondary Root Cause The Request Additional Documents loop Multiple requests especially in Case 2005 cause significant delays The duration of the Request phase waiting for docs is the main contributor to lead time Region No impact Resource No impact Performance is consistent per resource across complexity levels eg Lisa is fast on Low slow on High

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - PE
Text: Formulate Recommendations Mitigation 1 Address Complexity Can we simplify High complexity claims Or provide better guidance upfront to reduce the need for requests Mitigation 2 Reduce Request loops Case 2005 had 3 requests This implies the first requests werent clear or the documents provided were insufficient Suggestion Implement a checklist or validation step before sending requests Or One shot requests where all missing info is asked at once Mitigation 3 Optimize the waiting time If the delay is customer response can we automate reminders If internal can we prioritize Mitigation 4 Resource training Since resources handle all complexities maybe training on High complexity could speed up evaluation or request generation But the data shows resources are consistent so this is less critical than process changes The bottleneck is the process flow not the person
